name: The Recursive I Ching
license: Mixed — see each grammar's own `license` field (public-domain classical text; original synthesis/interpretation CC-BY-SA-4.0)

branches.name: Primary Sources — the classical text itself, in translation
Synthesis — cross-lens readings of the 64 hexagrams
Readings — thematic contemplative practices, not interpretation sets

grammars.name: The 64 Hexagrams
grammars.blurb: The Judgment, Image, and all six line texts for each of the 64 hexagrams, in condensed English, with a short interpretation gloss per hexagram. This is the flagship reading text of The Recursive I Chi…
grammars.year_label: Zhou I Ching, compiled c. 1046–256 BCE · this English condensation's own translation lineage is undocumented

grammars.name: The Repair I Ching: A Contemplative Practice
grammars.blurb: A meta-grammar that weaves the I Ching into the Repair framework. Twelve hexagrams — one for each chapter of 'Repair: An Open Source Book' — chosen not to predict but to reperceive. Each hexagram illu…

grammars.name: Three Lenses for Viewing the 64 Hexagrams
